# UC-22.2.6 · NIS2 Risk Analysis and Information System Security Policy Evidence

## Description

Article 21(2)(a) requires documented risk analysis and information security policies. This use case continuously validates that organisational risk posture is tracked, risk treatments are assigned owners, and security policy coverage aligns with critical asset inventory — producing auditable evidence that risk management is an operational process, not a one-time exercise.

## Value

Gives the responsible team auditor-ready evidence for NIS2 compliance by automating regulatory compliance evidence using indexes risk and sourcetypes stash. Replaces manual evidence gathering with a continuous Splunk search that produces structured, time-stamped artefacts suitable for regulatory examination and internal audit review.

## Implementation

(1) Populate ES asset and identity frameworks with NIS2-scoped systems; (2) ensure risk-generating correlation searches are active for all critical asset categories; (3) flag `UNASSIGNED` owners as governance gaps requiring remediation; (4) schedule weekly to generate evidence of continuous risk monitoring; (5) export as PDF for audit evidence pack. Indexes required: `index=risk`; Sourcetypes: `sourcetype=stash`; Lookups: `asset_lookup_by_str`.

## Detailed Implementation

Prerequisites
• Install and configure the required add-on or app: Splunk Enterprise Security (Splunkbase 263), Splunk Common Information Model Add-on (Splunkbase 1621).
• Ensure the following data sources are available: `index=risk` `sourcetype="stash"` (risk_object, risk_object_type, risk_score, source), asset/identity lookups, `_audit` index.
• For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 — Configure data collection
(1) Populate ES asset and identity frameworks with NIS2-scoped systems; (2) ensure risk-generating correlation searches are active for all critical asset categories; (3) flag `UNASSIGNED` owners as governance gaps requiring remediation; (4) schedule weekly to generate evidence of continuous risk monitoring; (5) export as PDF for audit evidence pack.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=risk sourcetype="stash" earliest=-30d@d
| stats latest(risk_score) as current_risk, max(risk_score) as peak_risk, dc(source) as contributing_detections by risk_object, risk_object_type
| lookup asset_lookup_by_str key AS risk_object OUTPUT category, priority, owner
| fillnull value="UNASSIGNED" owner category
| where owner="UNASSIGNED" OR current_risk > 50
| sort - current_risk
| table risk_object, risk_object_type, category, owner, current_risk, peak_risk, contributing_detections
```

Understanding this SPL

**NIS2 Risk Analysis and Information System Security Policy Evidence (Art. 21(2)(a))** — Article 21(2)(a) requires documented risk analysis and information security policies. This use case continuously validates that organisational risk posture is tracked, risk treatments are assigned owners, and security policy coverage aligns with critical asset inventory — producing auditable evidence that risk management is an operational process, not a one-time exercise.

Documented **Data sources**: `index=risk` `sourcetype="stash"` (risk_object, risk_object_type, risk_score, source), asset/identity lookups, `_audit` index. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): Splunk Enterprise Security (Splunkbase 263), Splunk Common Information Model Add-on (Splunkbase 1621). The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: risk; **sourcetype**: stash. That sourcetype matches what this use case lists under Data sources.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Scopes the data: index=risk, sourcetype="stash", time bounds. Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
• `stats` aggregates events by **risk_object, risk_object_type**, producing one row per unique combination for trend analysis and threshold evaluation.
• Enriches events using `lookup` (lookup definition + optional OUTPUT fields).
• Fills null values with `fillnull`.
• Filters the current rows with `where owner="UNASSIGNED" OR current_risk > 50` — typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.
• Orders rows with `sort` — combine with `head`/`tail` for top-N patterns.

Output fields: category, contributing_detections, current_risk, owner, peak_risk, risk_object, risk_object_type.

Step 3 — Validate
Compare with the ES Risk framework views (index=risk) and the ServiceNow GRC console for the same period so risk scores and asset fields line up.


Step 4 — Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Table (risk objects without owners), Bar chart (risk by category), Single value (unassigned assets), Line chart (risk trend over 30 days).

## SPL

```spl
index=risk sourcetype="stash" earliest=-30d@d
| stats latest(risk_score) as current_risk, max(risk_score) as peak_risk, dc(source) as contributing_detections by risk_object, risk_object_type
| lookup asset_lookup_by_str key AS risk_object OUTPUT category, priority, owner
| fillnull value="UNASSIGNED" owner category
| where owner="UNASSIGNED" OR current_risk > 50
| sort - current_risk
| table risk_object, risk_object_type, category, owner, current_risk, peak_risk, contributing_detections
```

## Visualization

Table (risk objects without owners), Bar chart (risk by category), Single value (unassigned assets), Line chart (risk trend over 30 days).
